title: Java Programming - Inheritance & Polymorphism Question Paper
prerequisites: Basic knowledge of Java syntax, classes, objects, and OOP concepts
instructions: Assume necessary imports are present unless specified
Pay attention to code formatting and indentation

questions.q: What is inheritance in Java? Why is it used?
questions.answer: Inheritance is an OOP concept where one class acquires properties and methods of another class. It promotes code reusability, establishes parent-child relationships, and supports method overriding for runtime polymorphism.

questions.q: Explain the role of access specifiers (private, default, protected, public) in inheritance.
questions.answer:
- private: Not inherited, accessible only within the same class
- default (no modifier): Inherited only within the same package
- protected: Inherited in same package and by subclasses even in different packages
- public: Inherited by all subclasses everywhere

questions.q: What is the purpose of the 'super' keyword in inheritance?
questions.answer: The 'super' keyword is used to refer to immediate parent class members. It can access parent class variables, methods, and constructors. It's used when child class overrides parent methods or to call parent constructor.

questions.q: What is method overriding? What are its rules?
questions.answer: Method overriding occurs when a subclass provides a specific implementation of a method already defined in parent class. Rules: same method name, same parameters, same or covariant return type, cannot have weaker access modifier.

questions.q: How does constructor work in inheritance?
questions.answer: In inheritance, the parent class constructor is called first (using super() implicitly or explicitly), then the child class constructor executes. If parent lacks a no-arg constructor, child must explicitly call super with parameters.

questions.q: What is an abstract class in Java?
questions.answer: An abstract class is a class declared with 'abstract' keyword that cannot be instantiated. It can contain both abstract methods (without body) and concrete methods (with body). It serves as a blueprint for subclasses.

questions.q: List the properties of an abstract class.
questions.answer:
1. Cannot be instantiated
2. Can have constructors (called when subclass is instantiated)
3. Can have abstract and non-abstract methods
4. Can have instance variables, static members
5. Can extend only one class (abstract or concrete)
6. Can implement multiple interfaces

questions.q: What is an abstract method? When must it be implemented?
questions.answer: An abstract method is a method declared without a body (implementation) using 'abstract' keyword. It must be implemented by the first concrete subclass. Abstract methods can only exist in abstract classes.

questions.q: What is runtime polymorphism (dynamic method dispatch) in Java?
questions.answer: Runtime polymorphism is when a parent class reference refers to a child class object, and the overridden method of the child class is called at runtime based on the actual object type, not reference type.

questions.q: Can we declare static methods in an abstract class? Explain.
questions.answer: Yes, abstract classes can have static methods. Static methods belong to the class, not objects. They can be called using the abstract class name, cannot be overridden (only hidden), and can have implementation.

questions.q: Explain the role of access specifiers/modifiers in inheritance with a complete example showing all four types.
Explain the use of 'super' keyword with examples for variables, methods, and constructors.
Explain method overriding in detail with rules and a complete example.
Explain constructor chaining in inheritance with examples. What happens if parent doesn't have default constructor?
Explain abstract classes in detail with properties and examples.
Explain runtime polymorphism (dynamic method dispatch) with a complete example.
Explain in detail: (a) Types of inheritance in Java (b) Why doesn't Java support multiple inheritance? (c) How to achieve multiple inheritance using interfaces?
Explain static members in inheritance and abstract classes. Include static variables, static methods, static blocks, and their behavior with examples.
Can we instantiate an abstract class? Explain with examples and alternative approaches.
Write a Java program demonstrating multilevel inheritance with a real-world example (e.g., Vehicle -> Car -> ElectricCar). Include method overriding, super keyword usage, and constructor chaining.
questions.hint: Create three levels with appropriate constructors and method overriding

questions.q: Write a Java program demonstrating abstract class with multiple concrete implementations. Create an abstract class 'Employee' with abstract methods calculateSalary() and displayInfo(). Implement it for FullTimeEmployee, PartTimeEmployee, and ContractEmployee classes. Demonstrate runtime polymorphism.
questions.hint: Use abstract class with different salary calculation strategies
